title: SR ajv -r flag workflow normalization — close FU-SR-AJV-COMPILE-INVOCATION-NORMALIZATION-1
description: Normalizes ajv-cli invocations across SR workflow files to consistently include -r flag for external $ref resolution. Surfaced as gap in PR #106 (Item B vpcPeerings v1.2.0, first negative case) and re-surfaced in PR #119 (TRUTH-DELIVERED #3 dispatch authoring miss, second negative case). FU was filed in PR #115; this deliverable closes it after audit-and-fix. Substrate-read of all workflow files at current main HEAD; surgical edits to invocations missing -r where the target schema has external $ref dependencies; in-PR FU closure (filed-and-closed pattern per PR #102/#108 precedent).

outputs.description: Audit `.github/workflows/roadmap-sync.yml` for ajv-cli invocations missing -r flag against schemas with external $ref dependencies. Per partial substrate read in prior session turn: lines 35 (roadmap.schema.json), 40 (venture-summary.schema.json), and 45-65 (decision-log, dependencies, boundaries, extensions, feature) — some have -r, some don't. Step 1 of dispatch re-verifies against current main HEAD substrate. For each invocation that needs -r: add `-r schemas/_definitions/forbidden-audit-paint-names.schema.json` (and any other external ref the target schema has per its $ref directives). For roadmap.schema.json line 35: verify whether it has external $refs — if no, document why no -r needed (substrate honesty); if yes, add. Match canonical pattern: `npx -y ajv-cli@5.0.0 validate -s <schema> -r <external-ref-schemas> -d <instance> --spec=draft7 --strict=false -c ajv-formats`.
acceptanceCriteria: All ajv-cli invocations in roadmap-sync.yml that target schemas with external $ref dependencies include the -r flag
For each invocation NOT requiring -r (target schema has no external $refs), inline yaml comment explains why
YAML syntax valid (yamllint pass or python -c 'import yaml; yaml.safe_load(open(...))' exit 0)
git diff scoped to exactly the invocation lines per memory #18 (no unrelated whitespace, no unrelated comment churn)

outputs.description: Close FU-SR-AJV-COMPILE-INVOCATION-NORMALIZATION-1 in .roadmap/follow-up-register.json per filed-and-closed pattern (PR #102/#108 precedent). Append closedAt + closedBy + closingDeliverable fields to FU entry. Bump meta.lastUpdated. Regenerate indexes (byStatus.open count -1, byStatus.closed count +1).
acceptanceCriteria: indexes.byStatus.open count decremented by 1, indexes.byStatus.closed count incremented by 1
ajv validates follow-up-register.json against follow-up-register.schema.json with -r luhtech-enums.schema.json flag, exit 0

outputs.description: Deliverable instance for this PR lands at S4-authored at schemas/_instances/deliverables/d-2026-06-04-sr-ajv-r-flag-workflow-normalization.json. Per memory #20 substrate-cleanup-as-you-go.
acceptanceCriteria: Deliverable instance lands at canonical path
ajv validates against deliverable.schema.json v1.1.0 with -r forbidden-audit-paint-names schema, exit 0

preMerge: Step 1 substrate read verifies which schemas referenced in roadmap-sync.yml have external $refs (not assumed from memory)
ajv-cli@5.0.0 validates follow-up-register.json against canonical schema with -r luhtech-enums.schema.json, exit 0
ajv-cli@5.0.0 validates this deliverable instance against deliverable.schema.json v1.1.0 with -r forbidden-audit-paint-names, exit 0
git diff --stat scoped to exactly 3 files
PR CI green; --admin merge forbidden per memory #15
All CI workflows (including the modified roadmap-sync.yml) pass on this PR — proves the normalization itself doesn't break anything
postMerge: roadmap-sync.yml canonical ajv pattern on main

transitions.trigger: Spec authored after partial substrate read in prior session turn surfaced ajv -r flag gaps in roadmap-sync.yml. Second-negative-case proof in PR #119 (TRUTH-DELIVERED dispatch authoring miss caught by agent REFUSE-AND-SURFACE) confirmed the FU is ripe NOW. Per Erik direction 2026-06-04 'lets address the workflow normalization, agreed'. Dispatch composes WITHOUT pre-baked surgical edits — Step 1 of execution does fresh substrate-read against current main HEAD 809d6d8 (post-PR #120) before any writes, per memory #6 substrate-before-write.

notes: Closes FU-SR-AJV-COMPILE-INVOCATION-NORMALIZATION-1 (filed in PR #115). Two negative-case proofs this session — PR #106 Item B vpcPeerings dispatch failure + PR #119 TRUTH-DELIVERED dispatch authoring miss. Spec design: dispatch does NOT prescribe specific line edits — Step 1 reads current substrate verbatim, agent reports findings, Erik approves line-by-line edits in Step 2 before any writes. Substrate-before-write per memory #6. Three other ripe-ish FUs (FU-SR-SCHEMA-VERSION-BUMP-REFS-REBUILD-DISPATCH-PATTERN-1 author-new, FU-RULE-PROJECTION-CONTRACT-F5-FUSION-AUTOMATED-1 design-decision-class) are explicitly out of scope for this PR per memory #18 branch scope-lock — each requires its own deliverable with its own substrate-read. Per memory #10 never fabricate values: the exact line-by-line edits to roadmap-sync.yml are deferred to Step 1 substrate-read; the spec acceptance criteria describe the canonical post-state, not pre-prescribed diffs.
